name: Updating phone number in /settings propagates to the next public-quote email signature
description: Diego updates his contact phone in /settings. The next quote he sends should include the new number in both the email body's contact line and the public-quote page's contact card. Existing already-sent quotes don't change (they're a record of what was sent at that time), but new sends and re-sends use the latest profile.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: Old number was a Google Voice line; switched to a real Verizon line. Wants new quotes to carry the new number.

steps.expect: Settings UI re-renders with formatted '(512) 555-9999'; save lands; toast confirms

steps.action: Returns to /assistant, drafts a new quote, sends
steps.expect: Outbound email body contact-line carries '(512) 555-9999'; public-quote /q/<id> renders the new number
Old public quote still shows the original (512) 555-0000 (snapshot semantics: customer's record of what was sent)

evaluationCriteria.check: Phone formatting consistent across email + public page (uses fmtPhone)
User trusts the change took effect — visible everywhere they expect
Profile is read at email-render time for new sends; public-quote page joins fresh profile, but the EMAIL HTML stored in the audit trail uses what was current at send time
Phone number rendered as a tel: link with E.164 form so the customer can tap to call on mobile
